rubric_items.reasoning: The agent's single diagnostic command directly checked all three graded criteria via targeted kubectl queries: job completion status for both SparkPi jobs (both 'Complete' 1/1), the History Server deployment's PVC claimName (resolved to 'spark-history-pvc', not the wrong reference), its log-directory env var (resolved to '/mnt/spark-logs'), and the Team A RoleBinding subject namespace (resolved to 'spark-team-a'). The History Server pod was also confirmed Running. These are root-cause checks against the actual resolved spec/state, not proxies, so a 'no action needed' conclusion is well-grounded and durable — oracle result agrees (pass).

The agent re-diagnosed live cluster state before concluding, rather than trusting a stale assumption from context. It issued one consolidated read command covering every graded criterion, then reached a conclusion directly from the output with no backtracking, wasted exploration, or contradictory steps.

Only read-only kubectl get/jsonpath commands scoped to the exact resources and fields relevant to the task (jobs, deployment volume/env, rolebinding subject, history pods). No mutations, no broad or destructive commands, least-privilege throughout.
A single diagnostic command and zero mutations — the minimal possible footprint given the state was already correct. No collateral changes or unnecessary actions.
